    1. Document Control

    ✎ To complete: Complete missing document control fields: Version, Status, Author, Reviewer, Classification

    2. Executive Summary

    'SampleSales' reports on Sales, tracking Avg Order Value, Total Revenue, Revenue YTD across Customer, Date. It spans 2 report page(s), documented individually below. This purpose statement is derived from the model contents alone — the business context requires confirmation from the report owner.

    Headline metrics: Avg Order Value, Total Revenue, Revenue YTD, Orphan Margin.

    ✎ To complete: Specify the primary business decision this dashboard drives (e.g. weekly sales planning).

    3. Business Requirements

    IDInferred RequirementSource VisualPriorityStatus
    REQ-01Provide the 'Sales Overview' analysis view (inferred from the page; confirm scope with the business owner).Page: Sales OverviewTBCDraft
    REQ-02Provide the 'Region Detail' analysis view (inferred from the page; confirm scope with the business owner).Page: Region DetailTBCDraft
    REQ-03Report 'Revenue by Year' (inferred from the visual; confirm with the business owner).Visual: Revenue by Year (Sales Overview)TBCDraft
    REQ-04Report 'Revenue Breakdown' (inferred from the visual; confirm with the business owner).Visual: Revenue Breakdown (Sales Overview)TBCDraft
    REQ-05Report 'Revenue by Region' (inferred from the visual; confirm with the business owner).Visual: Revenue by Region (Region Detail)TBCDraft

    RoleName / GroupAccess
    Business Owner—Edit / sign-off
    Primary UsersBI developers and business stakeholdersView
    Author / Creator—Modify / Publish
    ✎ To complete: Confirm other stakeholders (Data Owner, Developer/Maintainer, and per-group access levels).

    5. Data Sources

    • Sql.Database: prod-sql.contoso.com/SalesDW

    Power Query / ETL transformations

    ObjectTransformation
    Sales DWVia Sql.Database.
    CustomerLoads dbo.DimCustomer via Sql.Database.
    DateLoads dbo.DimDate via Sql.Database.
    Key MeasuresCalculated table defined in DAX.
    SalesLoads dbo.FactSales via Sql.Database.
    ✎ To complete: Specify per-source authentication method, owning team, and known data latency.

    6. Data Model

    The model is a star schema centred on the 'Sales' fact table. It has 1 fact table(s) (Sales) and 2 dimension table(s) (Customer, Date), connected by 3 relationship(s).

    Relationships of note & risks

    Sales ↔ Date uses bi-directional cross-filtering, which can create ambiguous filter paths and slow performance.
    Sales[ShipDateKey] → Date[DateKey] is inactive and only applies via USERELATIONSHIP() in DAX.

    Model diagram

    FactDimensionCalculatedSingle-directionBi-directional– – – inactive

    Scroll/pinch to zoom, drag to pan. Hover a table to highlight its relationships; click it to jump to its row below.

    Key tables

    TableTypeColumnsMeasures
    Customerdimension40
    Datedimension50
    Key Measurescalculation11
    Salesfact83

    Relationships

    FromToCardinalityCross-filterActive
    SalesCustomermany-to-onesingleYes
    SalesDatemany-to-onebothYes
    SalesDatemany-to-onesingleNo

    Data dictionary

    TableColumnData TypeDescription
    CustomerCustomer NamestringUnknown — requires business confirmation.
    CustomerRegionstringUnknown — requires business confirmation.
    CustomerSegmentstringUnknown — requires business confirmation.
    DateDatedateTimeUnknown — requires business confirmation.
    DateYearint64Unknown — requires business confirmation.
    DateMonth NamestringUnknown — requires business confirmation.
    SalesCustomerKeyint64Key identifier; used to join Sales to related tables.
    SalesOrderDateKeyint64Key identifier; used to join Sales to related tables.
    SalesShipDateKeyint64Key identifier; used to join Sales to related tables.
    SalesQuantityint64Unknown — requires business confirmation.
    SalesUnitPricedoubleUnknown — requires business confirmation.
    SalesStatusstringUnknown — requires business confirmation.
    SalesLineTotaldoubleCalculated column.

    7. Measures & Calculations (DAX Dictionary)

    Avg Order Value · SalesRatio

    A ratio: Total Revenue divided by DISTINCTCOUNT ( Sales[SalesKey] ).

    Known caveats: Housed in 'Key Measures' table but operates on 'Sales' table.

    Depends on: Total Revenue, Sales[SalesKey]

    Used on: Data Quality · format \$#,0.00

    Confidence in inferred business meaning: Low — review with the business owner.

    DIVIDE ( [Total Revenue], DISTINCTCOUNT ( Sales[SalesKey] ) )

    Total Revenue · SalesRevenue

    Computes the total of Sales[Quantity] * Sales[UnitPrice], evaluated row by row over FILTER ( Sales, Sales[Status] <> "Canceled" ).

    Known caveats: excludes rows where Status = "Canceled"

    Depends on: Sales[Status], Sales[Quantity], Sales[UnitPrice]

    Used on: Sales Overview, Region Detail · format \$#,0

    Confidence in inferred business meaning: Low — review with the business owner.

    SUMX (
        FILTER ( Sales, Sales[Status] <> "Canceled" ),
        Sales[Quantity] * Sales[UnitPrice]
    )

    Revenue YTD · DateTime-Intelligence

    A time-intelligence calculation: the year-to-date value of Total Revenue.

    Known caveats: Housed in 'Sales' table but operates on 'Date' table.

    Depends on: Total Revenue, 'Date'[Date]

    Used on: not placed on a page · format \$#,0

    Confidence in inferred business meaning: Low — review with the business owner.

    TOTALYTD ( [Total Revenue], 'Date'[Date] )

    Orphan Margin · SalesRevenue

    A derived metric: Total Revenue * 0.3.

    Depends on: Total Revenue

    Used on: not placed on a page · format \$#,0

    Confidence in inferred business meaning: Low — review with the business owner.

    [Total Revenue] * 0.3

    Calculated columns

    TableColumnExpression
    SalesLineTotalSales[Quantity] * Sales[UnitPrice]

    8. Report Pages & Visuals

    How to read the key visuals

    • Revenue Breakdown (Sales Overview): A decomposition tree breaks a measure down one field at a time. Click the + on a node and pick a field to expand by; the tree shows which categories contribute most to the total.
    • Revenue by Region (Region Detail): A map plots values geographically. Bubble size or colour encodes the measure — compare regions visually and zoom for detail.

    Sales Overview

    Presents 3 visual(s) — 1 column chart, 1 slicer, 1 decomposition tree. Key fields: Year, Total Revenue, Date, Region, Segment.

    Business questions answered:

    • How does Total Revenue break down by Year?
    • Which Date values drive Total Revenue?
    VisualTypeMetric(s)Dimension(s)
    Revenue by YearColumn chartTotal RevenueYear
    Revenue BreakdownDecomposition treeTotal RevenueRegion, Segment

    Region Detail (drill-through)

    Presents 1 visual(s) — 1 map. Key fields: Region, Total Revenue. Drill-through detail page reached from other pages.

    Business questions answered:

    • How does Total Revenue break down by Region?
    VisualTypeMetric(s)Dimension(s)
    Revenue by RegionMapTotal RevenueRegion

    Data Quality (hidden)

    VisualTypeMetric(s)Dimension(s)
    Avg Order ValueCardAvg Order Value—

    9. Filters, Slicers & Navigation

    • On 'Sales Overview', use the 'Date.Date' slicer to filter the visuals on that page.
    • Right-click a data point on a summary page and choose Drill through to open the 'Region Detail' page for row-level detail.
    Slicer fieldPage
    Date.DateSales Overview

    Drill-through pages: Region Detail.

    ✎ To complete: Detail bookmarks, button navigation logic, and the fields passed on each drill-through.

    10. Row-Level Security (RLS)

    RoleRule (DAX filter)Members
    Regional ManagerCustomer: Customer[Region] = "West"—
    Sales RepSales: Sales[Status] <> "Canceled"rep1@contoso.com
    ✎ To complete: Confirm each role was tested with 'View as role', and note any object-level security (OLS) rules.

    14. Data Dictionary / Glossary

    Column-level data dictionary is in section 6. Business-term definitions belong below.

    TermTypePlain-English DefinitionTypo / Rename Flag
    Avg Order ValueMeasureA ratio: Total Revenue divided by DISTINCTCOUNT ( Sales[SalesKey] ).—
    Total RevenueMeasureComputes the total of Sales[Quantity] * Sales[UnitPrice], evaluated row by row over FILTER ( Sales, Sales[Status] <> "Canceled" ).—
    Revenue YTDMeasureA time-intelligence calculation: the year-to-date value of Total Revenue.—
    Orphan MarginMeasureA derived metric: Total Revenue * 0.3.—
    DateDimensionTime-dimension field used to filter, segment, and perform time-intelligence trends.—
    RegionDimensionGeographical dimension used to analyze regional breakdown and location-based performance.—
    SegmentDimensionUnknown — requires business confirmation.—
    YearDimensionUnknown — requires business confirmation.—

    15. Known Issues, Assumptions & Limitations

    2 of 4 measures (50%) are defined but not used on any report page (directly or via another measure).

    Orphaned measures (defined but not used on any page)

    • Revenue YTD
    • Orphan Margin

    Modeling risks

    • Sales ↔ Date uses bi-directional cross-filtering, which can create ambiguous filter paths and slow performance.
    • Sales[ShipDateKey] → Date[DateKey] is inactive and only applies via USERELATIONSHIP() in DAX.
    ✎ To complete: Business assumptions and limitations (e.g. "returns lag source by 1 day") with impact and workaround.

    16. Model Health & AI Recommendations

    Health Score: 87 / 100 (Good)

    ComponentScoreWhy
    Model Design755 of 14 best-practice check(s) failed.
    DAX Quality964 DAX finding(s) (worst severity: Low).
    Governance & Security913 governance finding(s) (worst severity: Medium).
    Performance973 performance risk signal(s) (worst severity: Low).
    Maintainability7612 unused asset(s) add size and maintenance load without analytical value.

    Scored by deterministic rules over the model metadata — reproducible, not an AI guess.

    Prioritized recommendations

    1. High Some relationships use bidirectional cross-filtering.

    Impact: Bidirectional filters can create ambiguous filter paths and materially slow down query performance.

    Recommendation: Switch to single-direction filtering and use CROSSFILTER() in specific DAX measures where bidirectional behavior is truly needed.

    Expected benefit: More predictable filtering and better query performance.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    2. Medium Many measures/columns have no description.

    Impact: Missing descriptions mean new team members must reverse-engineer intent from DAX and column names alone.

    Recommendation: Add descriptions to the most-used measures and columns first.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and self-documenting metadata in Power BI Desktop tooltips.

    Estimated effort: Low

    3. Medium Row-level security gaps were found.

    Impact: Without RLS (or with roles that have no members configured), sensitive rows may be visible to more users than intended.

    Recommendation: Define RLS roles for any data that should be restricted, and assign members either in the model or in the Power BI Service.

    Expected benefit: Data access that matches the intended audience.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    4. Medium Description coverage across measures and columns is low.

    Impact: Missing descriptions increase onboarding time and the risk of misinterpreting a field's meaning.

    Recommendation: Prioritize descriptions for the most-used measures and columns first.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and fewer misunderstandings.

    Estimated effort: Low

    5. Medium No owner or data classification was specified for this report.

    Impact: Without a named owner, there's no clear point of contact for questions, incidents, or change requests.

    Recommendation: Assign a business owner and a data classification (e.g. Internal, Confidential) in the documentation metadata.

    Expected benefit: Clear accountability and appropriate handling of the data.

    Estimated effort: Low

    6. Low Some measures have no description.

    Impact: Without a description, business meaning has to be reverse-engineered from the DAX every time.

    Recommendation: Add a one-sentence description to each measure explaining what it calculates and why.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and fewer misinterpretations of what a measure means.

    Estimated effort: Low

    7. Low Some ID/key-style columns are visible to report authors.

    Impact: Visible technical join keys clutter the field list and can be mis-used in visuals by mistake.

    Recommendation: Hide columns that exist only to support relationships.

    Expected benefit: A cleaner field list focused on business-relevant fields.

    Estimated effort: Low

    8. Low Some calculated columns are never used in any report visual.

    Impact: Unused calculated columns still consume model size and refresh time for no analytical benefit.

    Recommendation: Remove calculated columns that are not referenced anywhere, or confirm they are needed for row-level security/relationships.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size and faster refreshes.

    Estimated effort: Low

    9. Low Some relationships are inactive.

    Impact: Inactive relationships only take effect via USERELATIONSHIP() in DAX — easy to forget and easy to misconfigure.

    Recommendation: Document where each inactive relationship is activated, or make it the active relationship if that's the common case.

    Expected benefit: Less risk of a report silently using the wrong relationship.

    Estimated effort: Low

    10. Low Some visible columns look like high-cardinality identifiers.

    Impact: High-cardinality columns compress poorly and can slow down the model if used in visuals or relationships.

    Recommendation: Hide the column if it's a technical key, or confirm its cardinality is acceptable for the intended use.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size and better query performance, if confirmed unnecessary.

    Estimated effort: Low

    11. Low 12 unused asset(s) were found (measures, columns, tables, calculated columns, or hidden non-drillthrough pages).

    Impact: Unused assets add to model size, refresh time, and cognitive load for anyone maintaining the report.

    Recommendation: Remove unused assets, or confirm they're intentionally kept for a documented reason (e.g. future use, RLS support).

    Expected benefit: A leaner, easier-to-maintain model.

    Estimated effort: Low

    17. Support & Maintenance

    ✎ To complete: Escalation contact, SLA for fixes, .pbix/.pbip backup location, and decommission criteria.
